The cluster center for C1 is calculated by averaging the x and y coordinates of points (0,6) and (6,0) which results in (3,3) while the center for C2 is found by averaging (2,2), (4,4), and (6,6) to get (4,4) and the center for C3 is derived from averaging (5,5) and (7,7) yielding (6,6) so the correct option is A.
******
Option A is the only choice that matches the computed means where C1 equals (3,3), C2 equals (4,4), and C3 equals (6,6) because the arithmetic mean of the x values in C1 is 3 and the mean of the y values is 3 while the means for C2 are both 4 and the means for C3 are both 6.
